description: Business-domain MOLT Aim blocks. Aim answers: 'What are we trying to achieve?' Each block describes the desired real-world business outcome — what success looks like when this commercial reasoning path completes.
usage: Select the Aim block that best describes the intended business outcome. Aim governs what the response is ultimately trying to accomplish in a commercial context.

REVENUE_GROWTH.description: Aims oriented toward increasing commercial income

blocks.outcome: To close the period at or above the committed revenue number
examples: achieving quarterly ARR target
closing the year at plan
hitting the monthly bookings goal

blocks.outcome: To increase annual recurring revenue through new business and expansion
examples: adding net new ARR above churn
expanding ARR in existing accounts
growing ARR from a new product tier

blocks.outcome: To close larger individual contracts by selling more scope or value
examples: upselling to enterprise tier
adding multi-year prepay
bundling services with software

blocks.outcome: To maintain a healthy volume of qualified opportunities relative to quota
examples: achieving 3x pipeline coverage
ensuring every rep has enough qualified deals
filling early-stage pipeline before end of quarter

blocks.outcome: To generate income from sources that did not previously exist in the business
examples: launching a professional services offering
monetizing a previously free feature
creating a marketplace revenue stream

CUSTOMER_OUTCOMES.description: Aims oriented toward customer satisfaction, retention, and advocacy

blocks.outcome: To keep existing customers renewing and engaged, reducing churn
examples: achieving net revenue retention above 110%
closing 90% of eligible renewals
reducing logo churn below 5% annually

blocks.outcome: To help the customer achieve the outcomes they purchased the product to deliver
examples: getting a customer to their first meaningful ROI
completing implementation within the agreed timeline
achieving the KPIs defined in the success plan

blocks.outcome: To raise NPS, CSAT, or equivalent scores by delivering a better experience
examples: moving NPS from 30 to 50 within 12 months
reducing post-onboarding CSAT complaints
improving support ticket satisfaction scores

blocks.outcome: To convert satisfied customers into active referrers and public champions
examples: generating referenceable customer accounts
securing case study participation
building a panel of reference customers for analyst briefings

blocks.outcome: To shorten the time between purchase and the customer realizing measurable benefit
examples: getting customers live in under 30 days
delivering first ROI in the first quarter
reducing implementation duration by 40%

MARKET_POSITION.description: Aims oriented toward competitive standing and brand strength

blocks.outcome: To increase the percentage of total available spend that flows to the business
examples: growing from 5% to 8% market share in a segment
displacing a competitor in key accounts
capturing share in a new geography

blocks.outcome: To be recognized as the leading player in a defined market category
examples: achieving Gartner Magic Quadrant leader placement
becoming the default choice for a buyer persona
owning the brand association for a problem category

blocks.outcome: To make the offering meaningfully distinct in ways that matter to buyers
examples: demonstrating a capability gap vs. the nearest competitor
winning on governance and auditability against alternatives
building a moat through proprietary data or technology

blocks.outcome: To increase unaided brand awareness among target buyers
examples: raising brand awareness score in ICP segment by 20%
getting the brand mentioned in industry analyst reports
achieving recognition at target conferences

blocks.outcome: To achieve initial traction in a new geographic or vertical market
examples: closing first 10 customers in a new country
landing reference accounts in a new vertical
establishing a beachhead in an adjacent segment

FINANCIAL_HEALTH.description: Aims oriented toward profitability, efficiency, and financial sustainability

blocks.outcome: To reach a state where revenue consistently exceeds costs
examples: reaching EBITDA breakeven
achieving gross margin targets
becoming cash flow positive

blocks.outcome: To lower the cost of acquiring and serving customers relative to the value they generate
examples: reducing CAC by 20%
improving LTV/CAC ratio above 3x
increasing gross margin through cost reduction

blocks.outcome: To increase the time the business can operate before requiring additional capital
examples: reducing burn rate below $500k/month
achieving 18 months of runway post-raise
cutting operating costs to extend to next milestone

blocks.outcome: To deliver the agreed earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortization
examples: meeting the board-approved EBITDA plan
returning to positive EBITDA after an investment cycle
improving EBITDA margin by 5 points year on year

blocks.outcome: To deploy available funds in the combination of activities that produces the highest return
examples: shifting spend from low-ROI channels to high performers
prioritizing product investment over headcount growth
retiring inefficient programs to fund a strategic bet

OPERATIONAL_EXCELLENCE.description: Aims oriented toward efficiency, quality, and reliable delivery

blocks.outcome: To deliver the same or better results with fewer resources or less time
examples: reducing cost per unit served
automating a manual process to free up headcount
cutting meeting overhead without losing coordination

blocks.outcome: To meet all contractual service level commitments to customers
examples: resolving 95% of P1 issues within SLA
meeting uptime SLA across all enterprise customers
responding to support tickets within committed time windows

blocks.outcome: To lower the rate of mistakes, rework, and defects in business processes
examples: reducing billing errors to under 0.1%
cutting data entry errors in order processing
improving compliance with the expense policy

blocks.outcome: To grow delivery capacity without increasing costs at the same rate
examples: doubling customer count without doubling support team
tripling transaction volume with the same infrastructure team
growing revenue per employee year on year

blocks.outcome: To complete commitments within agreed timeframes consistently
examples: shipping the product milestone on the committed date
completing client deliverables before contractual deadline
hitting the quarterly close on schedule

PEOPLE_TALENT.description: Aims oriented toward building, retaining, and developing a high-performing workforce

blocks.outcome: To fill all approved headcount with qualified candidates within the target timeframe
examples: closing all Q1 engineering headcount by end of quarter
filling a critical VP role within 60 days
hitting a headcount plan for a new office opening

blocks.outcome: To keep high-performing employees engaged and prevent attrition
examples: reducing top-quartile attrition below 5%
retaining all critical sales performers through a reset
maintaining key technical talent through a product pivot

blocks.outcome: To raise commitment, motivation, and satisfaction scores across the workforce
examples: improving engagement survey score by 10 points
reducing voluntary turnover below 12%
achieving eNPS above 30

blocks.outcome: To develop internal candidates who are ready to step into senior roles
examples: identifying and developing two internal VP candidates
ensuring every director has a successor identified
completing a high-potential leadership program

blocks.outcome: To give the organization the specific capability it lacks and needs to execute
examples: building an in-house AI engineering team
developing a sales team's enterprise discovery skills
training the ops team on a new ERP system

STRATEGIC_POSITIONING.description: Aims oriented toward long-term competitive advantage and strategic clarity

blocks.outcome: To align the entire organization on a clear, shared direction and set of priorities
examples: completing an annual strategy refresh with leadership alignment
communicating a pivot clearly to all employees
closing the gap between stated strategy and resource allocation

blocks.outcome: To create structural advantages that are difficult for competitors to replicate
examples: building a proprietary data asset
achieving network effects in a marketplace
establishing a dominant partner ecosystem

blocks.outcome: To raise capital that enables execution of a defined growth strategy
examples: closing a Series B to fund enterprise GTM build-out
securing a strategic investor with sector expertise
raising a bridge to reach a profitability milestone

blocks.outcome: To acquire a company or asset that accelerates strategic goals
examples: acquiring a competitor to consolidate market share
buying a team to close a critical technology gap
acquiring a customer base through a tuck-in acquisition

blocks.outcome: To reach a state where the business can be sold, listed, or transitioned successfully
examples: preparing clean financials and a clean cap table for an M&A process
achieving the ARR and growth rate for an IPO filing
completing a management buyout preparation

COMPLIANCE_RISK.description: Aims oriented toward meeting obligations and protecting the business from risk

blocks.outcome: To demonstrate full compliance to a regulator or certification body
examples: passing a GDPR audit with no findings
achieving SOC 2 Type II certification
clearing an SEC examination

blocks.outcome: To eliminate a known deviation from required legal or regulatory standards
examples: remediating a data handling gap flagged in an audit
updating a contract template to reflect new regulation
closing a HIPAA compliance finding

blocks.outcome: To minimize the financial and reputational risk from litigation or regulatory action
examples: resolving a contractual dispute before litigation
limiting liability through indemnity restructuring
proactively addressing a regulatory gray area

blocks.outcome: To secure legal ownership of proprietary assets and prevent unauthorized use
examples: filing patents before a public announcement
registering trademarks in key markets
enforcing IP rights against an infringer

blocks.outcome: To uphold board-level and regulatory governance requirements consistently
examples: maintaining an independent board with required committee structure
filing required corporate disclosures on time
documenting and following a board-approved policy framework

PARTNERSHIP_OUTCOMES.description: Aims oriented toward creating and extracting value from partnerships

blocks.outcome: To generate a defined volume of qualified opportunities via the partner channel
examples: achieving 20% of new pipeline from partners
activating a set of resellers to hit a sourced pipeline target
launching a referral program that generates 50 leads per quarter

blocks.outcome: To close a higher proportion of deals when working jointly with a partner
examples: improving partner-assisted win rate above 60%
closing a strategic deal with a SI partner
winning a competitive deal through cloud marketplace co-sell

blocks.outcome: To create a network of engaged partners who regularly generate business value
examples: activating 50 selling partners in 12 months
building a community of certified implementation partners
establishing a marketplace with active ISV participants

blocks.outcome: To establish a formalized partnership with a major player that creates structural advantage
examples: signing a strategic go-to-market agreement with a cloud provider
formalizing a technology integration partnership with a market leader
establishing a co-development agreement with a strategic vendor

blocks.aim: Reduce cost of sales via partners
blocks.outcome: To lower the average cost to acquire a customer by shifting volume to partner channels
examples: shifting 30% of deals to lower-cost partner channel
reducing direct sales headcount through channel leverage
achieving partner-assisted deals at 40% lower CAC

PRODUCT_SUCCESS.description: Aims oriented toward building and delivering products that achieve market traction

blocks.outcome: To reach a state where the product demonstrably meets the needs of a repeatable customer segment
examples: achieving 40%+ would-be-very-disappointed score on a Superhuman-style PMF survey
reaching a segment where organic word of mouth is growing
demonstrating consistent renewal and expansion in a defined ICP

blocks.outcome: To deliver committed product milestones within the planned timeframe and scope
examples: shipping a promised enterprise feature before a renewal deadline
delivering a roadmap quarter on time and within scope
meeting a committed launch date

blocks.outcome: To increase active usage of the product among the target user base
examples: growing daily active users by 30%
increasing feature adoption in the customer base
improving activation rate from signup to first key action

blocks.outcome: To prevent customers from leaving because of product gaps, bugs, or poor experience
examples: eliminating the most common product-cited churn reasons
reducing support escalations caused by product issues
improving stability and reducing critical bugs in production

blocks.aim: Win a competitive deal on product
blocks.outcome: To beat an alternative in a direct comparison on product capability, fit, or innovation
examples: winning an enterprise deal where the competitor had more features
demonstrating a differentiating capability in a bake-off
winning a POC evaluation against a named alternative

BRAND_REPUTATION.description: Aims oriented toward earning and protecting a strong market reputation

blocks.outcome: To shift how target buyers think and feel about the brand positively
examples: moving from unknown to recognized in a new segment
rebuilding trust after a public incident
improving analyst perception in a Gartner report

blocks.outcome: To secure favorable coverage in outlets and channels the target audience trusts
examples: landing a product launch story in a tier-one trade publication
generating analyst coverage for a new category
getting a founder feature in a business publication

blocks.outcome: To be recognized as a credible expert voice on a topic that matters to buyers
examples: publishing an original research report that earns media coverage
building a speaker presence at industry conferences
developing a podcast that becomes the reference in the category

blocks.outcome: To limit reputational damage and restore confidence during a public incident
examples: managing communications around a data breach
responding effectively to a negative viral moment
restoring customer trust after a major service outage

blocks.outcome: To attract top candidates by being known as a great place to work
examples: improving Glassdoor rating above 4.2
being recognized on a best-places-to-work list
generating qualified inbound job applications from brand pull

SCALE_EFFICIENCY.description: Aims oriented toward growing the business without growing costs proportionally

blocks.outcome: To replace manual effort with reliable automation in a repeatable business operation
examples: automating invoice generation and delivery
automating a first-line support triage
automating a routine compliance check

blocks.outcome: To grow revenue faster than headcount, improving organizational productivity
examples: growing revenue per FTE from $300k to $400k
scaling ARR 2x with the same commercial team
delivering more output per employee through tooling investment

blocks.outcome: To lower the cost of customer support without reducing satisfaction
examples: deflecting 40% of tickets through self-service content
reducing average handle time with AI-assisted support
shifting volume from high-cost channels to lower-cost digital

blocks.outcome: To create a consistent, documented process that new people can follow reliably
examples: standardizing the enterprise sales discovery process
creating a repeatable customer onboarding playbook
building a standard vendor evaluation framework

blocks.outcome: To shorten the average duration from job opening to accepted offer
examples: reducing time-to-hire from 60 to 30 days
standardizing interview process to remove bottlenecks
implementing sourcing automation to reduce sourcing time

TRANSFORMATION_OUTCOMES.description: Aims oriented toward organizational and business model transformation

blocks.outcome: To modernize core business processes and systems to operate effectively in a digital environment
examples: migrating from legacy ERP to cloud-native platform
moving from manual sales process to CRM-driven workflow
transitioning from paper-based to digital-first operations

blocks.outcome: To evolve from selling a single product to operating an extensible platform with ecosystem value
examples: building an API and marketplace around a core product
enabling third-party developers to build on the platform
generating ecosystem revenue alongside direct product revenue

blocks.outcome: To bring a newly acquired company into operations without disrupting either entity
examples: integrating systems and data within 12 months post-close
unifying GTM teams after a merger
consolidating support and customer success operations

blocks.outcome: To change the fundamental way the business creates and captures value
examples: transitioning from perpetual license to SaaS subscription
shifting from services-led to product-led growth
moving from a direct sales model to a PLG motion

blocks.outcome: To re-stabilize a team and restore high performance after a reorganization
examples: achieving full team productivity within 60 days of a reorg
aligning a newly merged team on shared goals and culture
restoring engagement scores after a workforce reduction

INVESTOR_STAKEHOLDER.description: Aims oriented toward satisfying investors, board, and key stakeholders

blocks.outcome: To complete a capital raise and receive funds from investors
examples: closing a Series A within 6 months of launch
completing a bridge round to extend runway
closing an oversubscribed round from strategic investors

blocks.outcome: To deliver the performance and governance standards the board has approved
examples: hitting the plan approved at the last board meeting
delivering a clean audit with no material findings
maintaining the governance commitments in the investor agreement

blocks.outcome: To generate financial returns for shareholders through growth or liquidity events
examples: achieving a valuation step-up at next round
delivering an M&A exit at target multiple
generating distributions to investors from operating profit

blocks.outcome: To build the business to the scale and metrics that justify a defined valuation
examples: growing to the ARR and growth rate that supports a Series C valuation
achieving the metrics required for an IPO at target price
meeting the acquisition price expectations of a strategic buyer

blocks.outcome: To keep existing investors informed and supportive through good and difficult periods
examples: communicating a missed quarter with credibility and a clear plan
maintaining constructive board relationships through a pivot
building investor trust through consistent reporting and follow-through

GTM_EXECUTION.description: Aims oriented toward effective go-to-market execution

blocks.outcome: To bring a new product to market in a way that generates immediate traction
examples: achieving target sign-ups in the first 30 days post-launch
generating press coverage and qualified leads from a launch event
landing first 10 enterprise customers in the launch quarter

blocks.outcome: To develop a predictable, documentable process that reliably converts qualified leads to customers
examples: codifying the enterprise sales playbook so all reps follow it
achieving predictable win rates from a defined sales stage process
reducing deal cycle variance through a standardized motion

blocks.outcome: To close a higher proportion of contested opportunities
examples: improving overall win rate from 25% to 35%
increasing win rate against the named competitor
improving technical evaluation win rate through better POC management

blocks.outcome: To shorten the time from initial opportunity to closed deal
examples: cutting average cycle from 90 to 60 days
accelerating SMB deals to under 30 days
removing a procurement bottleneck that was adding 3 weeks

blocks.outcome: To ensure every target account in the addressable market is being actively pursued
examples: assigning an SDR or AE to every ICP account in the territory
ensuring all named accounts are contacted at least once per quarter
achieving full coverage of a target industry vertical

LEARNING_IMPROVEMENT.description: Aims oriented toward improving the business through systematic learning

blocks.outcome: To understand why deals were won or lost and use that to improve future performance
examples: interviewing buyers in 10 recent losses to identify common themes
conducting a competitive win analysis to document differentiators
using win/loss data to revise the sales playbook

blocks.outcome: To assess whether a project or change delivered its intended outcome and what can be learned
examples: reviewing a CRM implementation against original success criteria
evaluating a marketing campaign against its objectives
reviewing a reorg against its stated goals 6 months later

blocks.outcome: To ensure the team has the information and skills needed to perform at a high level
examples: assessing sales skills gaps after a quota miss
identifying product knowledge gaps in the support team
finding and closing competitive intelligence gaps in marketing

blocks.outcome: To capture and systematize what the organization has learned so it persists beyond individuals
examples: documenting the sales playbook after a successful quarter
creating a customer success handbook from experienced CS reps
building a knowledge base from top-performing support resolutions

blocks.outcome: To make better choices by using more rigorous frameworks, better data, and clearer criteria
examples: implementing a structured product prioritization framework
using data to replace gut feel in hiring decisions
adopting a formal business case process for investment decisions

CULTURE_WELLBEING.description: Aims oriented toward organizational health and employee wellbeing

blocks.outcome: To create an environment where people consistently do great work and hold each other to high standards
examples: establishing a culture of direct feedback and accountability
building a high-transparency operating cadence
creating a values-aligned recognition program

blocks.outcome: To create conditions where people feel safe to speak up, take risks, and admit mistakes
examples: training managers on creating safe team environments
establishing a blameless post-mortem culture
reducing fear of speaking up in leadership meetings

blocks.outcome: To lower unsustainable workload and stress before it causes attrition and health problems
examples: implementing a sustainable meeting culture
setting clear work-hours expectations
identifying and addressing the root causes of team overload

blocks.outcome: To raise the standard of people management across the organization through training and accountability
examples: training all managers on performance conversations
establishing a manager effectiveness feedback process
identifying and developing the bottom quartile of managers

blocks.outcome: To build a workplace where people of all backgrounds can contribute fully and feel they belong
examples: achieving gender parity at director level and above
building inclusion into hiring and promotion decisions
reducing attrition gaps between demographic groups

DATA_INTELLIGENCE.description: Aims oriented toward using data to make the business smarter and faster

blocks.outcome: To embed data-informed decision making as the default across the organization
examples: moving from gut-feel to metric-based performance conversations
getting every team to report on KPIs weekly
making self-serve analytics available to all business leaders

blocks.aim: Achieve a single source of truth
blocks.outcome: To eliminate conflicting data and align the organization on one authoritative set of numbers
examples: resolving the conflict between finance and sales revenue figures
building a unified customer data platform
standardizing the definition of ARR across the company

blocks.outcome: To forecast customer actions — churn, expansion, support volume — with enough accuracy to act on
examples: building a churn prediction model with 80% accuracy
forecasting renewal outcomes 90 days in advance
predicting expansion opportunity with enough lead time to act

blocks.outcome: To reduce the variance between predicted and actual business outcomes
examples: improving sales forecast accuracy to within 5% of plan
reducing financial forecast miss rate
improving demand planning accuracy for operations

blocks.outcome: To determine through data analysis which investments will generate the greatest return
examples: identifying the product feature most correlated with retention
finding the marketing channel with the lowest CAC
identifying the customer segment with the highest LTV/CAC

SUSTAINABILITY_IMPACT.description: Aims oriented toward responsible business and long-term societal impact

blocks.outcome: To meet defined environmental commitments such as carbon, waste, or energy goals
examples: reaching carbon neutrality by a committed date
reducing operational energy consumption by 30%
eliminating single-use plastics from operations

blocks.outcome: To produce credible, complete disclosures that meet stakeholder expectations
examples: publishing an annual ESG report to GRI standards
completing a TCFD climate disclosure
achieving a target ESG rating from an external agency

blocks.outcome: To create measurable benefit for communities, employees, or customers beyond commercial gain
examples: launching a community investment program
committing 1% of ARR to social initiatives
achieving living wage certification for all employees

blocks.outcome: To embed responsible AI principles into how AI systems are built, governed, and deployed
examples: adopting a formal AI ethics policy
deploying AI with auditable governance via UMG
establishing an AI review board for high-stakes applications

blocks.outcome: To ensure the full supplier ecosystem meets ethical and environmental standards
examples: auditing tier-1 suppliers for labor practices
requiring environmental standards from key vendors
removing suppliers who fail ethical assessments

UMG_BUSINESS_AIMS.description: Aims specific to deploying UMG in commercial and organizational contexts

blocks.aim: Deploy a governed AI commercial agent
blocks.outcome: To put an auditable, policy-compliant AI agent into a business function that produces measurable results
examples: activating a governed AI SDR that books qualified meetings
deploying a governed support agent that resolves tickets within policy
launching a governed BI agent that delivers accurate executive reports

blocks.outcome: To ensure AI decisions in regulated business contexts are fully traceable and defensible
examples: producing a Decision Trace for every AI-assisted credit decision
generating auditable AI outputs for a financial services workflow
meeting a regulator's AI transparency requirements with UMG traces

blocks.outcome: To prevent AI agents from taking unauthorized commercial actions through governance
examples: blocking an AI from offering unauthorized discounts
preventing an AI from making contractual commitments outside scope
ensuring AI communications stay within legal and brand guidelines

blocks.aim: Scale AI-assisted operations without proportional risk
blocks.outcome: To grow the use of AI in business operations while keeping governance tight
examples: tripling AI-assisted transactions without relaxing compliance controls
expanding AI agent deployment across geographies under unified governance
growing AI coverage without increasing compliance overhead proportionally

blocks.outcome: To provide verifiable evidence of responsible AI deployment to customers, regulators, and investors
examples: publishing a governance report for enterprise customer due diligence
providing audit evidence to a regulator asking about AI use
presenting AI governance architecture to a board risk committee
